title: Preflight scaffolds credentials.json skeleton file
summary: When preflight runs, it creates ~/.golemic/<project>/credentials.json as an empty template with correct file permissions (0600), so users immediately know where and how to provide bot tokens without manual guessing or misconfiguration.

stakeholder_intent.actor: Developer setting up golemic on a new machine
stakeholder_intent.goal: Know the exact shape and location of the credentials file needed to run golemic
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Reduces setup friction, prevents misconfiguration (wrong path, wrong format, insecure permissions), and makes the setup flow self-documenting
stakeholder_intent.trigger: The developer runs `golemic preflight` for the first time in a newly cloned host repo with symlinked golemic
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: Preflight creates ~/.golemic/<project>/credentials.json with correct JSON structure and 0600 permissions, then tells the user exactly what to fill in

in_scope: Create ~/.golemic/<project>/ directory if it doesn't exist
Create credentials.json skeleton with dev_token and reviewer_token fields set to empty strings
Set file permissions to 0600 (owner read/write, no group/world access)
Implement idempotent behavior: never overwrite existing credentials.json
Add new preflight check step: checkCredentialsScaffolding()
Extract a shared writeFileAtomic() helper to avoid code duplication between config.json and credentials.json scaffolding
Update preflight output messages to guide users on what to fill in
Add unit tests for the new helper and check function
out_of_scope: Validating or parsing token content (validation is checkCredentials' responsibility)
Helping users generate GitHub PATs (documented separately, linked in checkCredentials help text)
Creating alternative deployment methods (env vars already supported by credentials loader)
Home directory discovery or migration logic

preconditions: Preflight is running from within a git repository (verified by checkGit)
User has write access to ~/.golemic/ or sufficient permissions for it to be created
Project name is valid per credentialValidator.ValidateProjectName (alphanumeric starting char, dot/hyphen/underscore allowed; no path traversal)
Architecture §2.9 contract is known: credentials.json location, permissions (0600), and field structure

business_rules.rule: credentials.json skeleton shall contain exactly two string fields: dev_token and reviewer_token, both empty
business_rules.outcome: File created with valid JSON matching credentialsFile struct

business_rules.rule: credentials.json file permissions shall be set to 0600 (owner read/write, no group/world access)
business_rules.outcome: Secret file is not world-readable or group-readable

business_rules.rule: Parent directory ~/.golemic/<project>/ shall be created with 0755 permissions if missing
business_rules.applies_when: Destination directory structure does not exist
business_rules.outcome: Directory hierarchy created and traversable by the user

business_rules.rule: Existing credentials.json shall never be overwritten, modified, or truncated
business_rules.applies_when: checkCredentialsScaffolding() is called and file already exists
business_rules.outcome: File left untouched; user must manually delete to reset

business_rules.rule: checkCredentialsScaffolding() shall execute after checkScaffolding() and before checkCredentials()
business_rules.outcome: Scaffolding check happens before validation check, mirroring repo-side config pattern

business_rules.rule: If credentials.json exists but has permissions >0600, preflight shall not fail the scaffolding check; checkCredentials() will enforce permission strictness
business_rules.applies_when: File exists from a previous run with wrong permissions
business_rules.outcome: Scaffolding check passes (file exists), validation check reports the permission violation

business_rules.rule: writeFileAtomic() helper shall be extracted and reused by both createConfig() and createCredentialsSkeleton()
business_rules.outcome: Single source of truth for atomic write, permission setting, and idempotency logic

then.outcome: File created with skeleton and 0600 perms; report Ok=false (FEHLT)
Idempotent; report Ok=true and proceed to validation
Do not modify file; let checkCredentials report permission violation

interfaces.name: writeFileAtomic (new shared helper)
interfaces.operation: writeFileAtomic(path string, content []byte, perms os.FileMode) error

inputs.name: path
constraints: Parent directory must be creatable by user

inputs.name: perms
constraints: Must be <= 0644 for config, exactly 0600 for credentials

outputs.body: File created at path with exact permissions

errors.message: File exists at path; idempotency requires caller to delete manually
Failed to create parent directory (permission denied or disk full)
Failed to write file (disk full, I/O error, or permission denied)

inputs.name: p.homeDir
constraints: Non-empty absolute path to user home directory

inputs.name: p.repoRoot
constraints: Non-empty absolute path; project name derived from base

outputs.body: Result{Name: 'Credentials Scaffolding', Ok: bool, Details: string}

errors.message: Project name fails regex validation; no traversal allowed
Failed to create credentials.json or parent directories (permission or I/O error)

state_changes.precondition: Directory does not exist or credentials.json does not exist

side_effects.description: Create ~/.golemic/<project>/ directory and credentials.json file on first preflight run
side_effects.idempotency: Does not overwrite existing credentials.json; directory and file creation are idempotent (no-op if they already exist)
side_effects.failure_policy: If directory creation fails, return error and do not create file; caller (preflight) aborts check chain

security.authentication: Preflight is run by the user locally; no authentication required
authorization_rules: User must have write permission to ~/.golemic/ or permission to create it
No elevated privileges required; uses user's own home directory
security.data_classification: Credentials.json skeleton is not sensitive until user fills it with actual tokens
audit_requirements: File creation logged via preflight stdout (OK or FEHLT message)

integration_points: Existing Result type and RunAll() orchestration method
architecture_constraints: Must follow existing preflight pattern: each check returns one Result
Must respect file permission contract from §2.9 (0600)
allowed_changes: Refactor existing createConfig() to call writeFileAtomic()
Insert checkCredentialsScaffolding() into RunAll() between checkScaffolding() and checkCredentials()
forbidden_changes: Do not modify credentials.Loader (validation is separate slcie)
Do not create files with permissions other than 0600 for credentials or 0755 for directories
dependencies: May reuse filepath.Join() for path construction
implementation_context.migration_strategy: Preflight is setup tool; no schema migration needed; existing ~/.golemic/ structures are left untouched
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Preflight is single-threaded, run once per machine setup; no concurrency concerns; repeated runs are idempotent

acceptance_scenarios.title: First preflight run creates credentials.json skeleton
given: User runs preflight for the first time
then: Message tells user: 'created ~/.golemic/<project>/credentials.json; fill in dev_token and reviewer_token'

acceptance_scenarios.title: Repeated preflight runs are idempotent
given: credentials.json already exists from a previous preflight run
then: File is not modified or overwritten

acceptance_scenarios.title: File permissions are correct at creation
when: writeFileAtomic() writes the file with perms=0600
then: File is not readable or writable by group or world

acceptance_scenarios.title: Parent directory is created if missing
when: createCredentialsSkeleton() determines path and calls writeFileAtomic()
then: All parent dirs are traversable by user

acceptance_scenarios.title: Check execution order places scaffolding before validation
Invalid project names are rejected
given: Project name contains invalid characters (e.g., '../', spaces, special chars)
when: checkCredentialsScaffolding() validates project name using credentials.ValidateProjectName()
then: No directory or file is created

acceptance_scenarios.title: writeFileAtomic() is reused by config scaffolding
given: Both config.json and credentials.json scaffolding are needed
when: createConfig() and createCredentialsSkeleton() both reuse writeFileAtomic()
then: Helper is called with perms=0644 for config.json
Helper is called with perms=0600 for credentials.json

test_cases: writeFileAtomic() creates file with exact permissions when path does not exist
writeFileAtomic() does not overwrite existing file; returns error
writeFileAtomic() creates parent directories with 0755 if missing
writeFileAtomic() returns error when parent directory creation fails
checkCredentialsScaffolding() creates credentials.json with correct skeleton content
checkCredentialsScaffolding() returns Ok=false (FEHLT) when file is created
checkCredentialsScaffolding() returns Ok=true when file already exists
checkCredentialsScaffolding() returns error for invalid project names
Preflight.RunAll() output includes checkCredentialsScaffolding() result in correct position
Idempotency: repeated preflight runs do not modify existing credentials.json
non_functional_requirements: File creation is atomic (no partial writes on interruption)
Permissions are set exactly (no umask interference)
definition_of_done: All test cases pass with ≥80% coverage of new code
Preflight integration test verified in golemic_e2e repo

decision_log.decision: Preflight should create credentials.json skeleton (not user manual creation)
decision_log.rationale: Reduces setup friction; users know exactly what file shape is needed and where to put it without manual guessing

decision_log.decision: credentials.json skeleton contains empty strings for dev_token and reviewer_token
decision_log.rationale: Matches credentialsFile struct definition in credentials.go; minimal valid JSON

decision_log.decision: File permissions are set to 0600 at creation and never changed
decision_log.rationale: Prevents accidental world-readable secret files; matches §2.9 architecture contract

decision_log.decision: Implementation is idempotent: never overwrite existing credentials.json
decision_log.rationale: Repeated preflight runs are safe; user must explicitly delete if they want a fresh template; aligns with §2.11

decision_log.decision: Extract shared writeFileAtomic() helper for both config.json and credentials.json scaffolding
decision_log.rationale: Reduces code duplication; centralizes file permission and idempotency logic; easier to test and maintain

decision_log.decision: New checkCredentialsScaffolding() runs before checkCredentials() in RunAll() sequence
decision_log.rationale: Mirrors config scaffolding pattern: scaffold creates template, validator checks contents

codebase_evidence.finding: Struct defines dev_token and reviewer_token string fields
Architecture specifies ~/.golemic/<project>/credentials.json with 0600 permissions and dev/reviewer token fields
Architecture mandates idempotency: worktrees remain on error, no auto-cleanup or overwrite
Existing repo-side scaffolding pattern shows how to create files idempotently
Preflight check orchestration pattern: each check returns Result{Name, Ok, Details}
Function available to validate project names and prevent path traversal
Existing unit test patterns for other checks provide template for testing new functions
